title: 1.1: Reports session acceptance versus rejection counts when multiple sessions approach max_sessions limit (e.g., 2 accepted, 2 rejected when limit is 2).
1.2: Reports quota value changes at each step during concurrent uploads (e.g., 8000 -> 16000 -> 31000 -> 46000 -> 56000 bytes).
1.3: Explains how quota checks serialize or race across simultaneous transfers (e.g., through mutex-protected database queries).
1.4: Identifies where quota coordination first manifests as runtime evidence (e.g., hasSpace function check, database query logs).
1.5: Reports what happens to partial temporary files when atomic upload is interrupted (e.g., .sftpgo-upload prefixed file deleted).
1.6: Reports quota accounting behavior after interrupted atomic upload (e.g., quota unchanged at 0 bytes, 0 files).

1.7: Reports actual log messages observed during concurrent session and quota burst (e.g., "authentication refused...too many open sessions", "quota exceed for user", "denying file write due to space limit").
1.8: Reports file system state during active transfers (e.g., .sftpgo-upload temporary files visible during upload).
1.9: Reports observable behavior when idle timeout terminates connection (e.g., "close idle connection, idle time: 4m49s", specific timeout duration)

1.10: Identifies observable log markers distinguishing successful upload completion from interrupted transfer (e.g., "atomic upload completed, rename" for success vs "atomic upload completed with error...delete temporary file" for interruption).
1.11: Explains how session management coordinates with other subsystems (e.g., global mutex-protected openConnections map).
1.12: Identifies where handoffs occur between subsystems (e.g., transfer start triggers hasSpace check, transfer end triggers UpdateUserQuota).
1.13: Reports observable differences between clean run versus contention scenario (e.g., clean shows only rename logs, contention shows refused/exceed/delete messages).
1.14: Reports upload success versus failure counts during quota enforcement test (e.g., 5 successful, 1 failed when file limit is 5).
